data.content: The tombstone has 7 frames in `libmonosgen-2.0.so` (BuildId: `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`). I'll try to fetch debug symbols from Microsoft's symbol server and symbolicate them.

arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; apt list --installed 2>/dev/null | grep -i llvm | head -5

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
# Try Microsoft symbol server - ELF buildid format
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
FILENAME="libmonosgen-2.0.so"

# Try various symbol server URL formats
echo "=== Trying symbol server ==="
for url in \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" \
  "https://symbols.nuget.org/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}" \
  "https://symbols.nuget.org/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug"; do
  echo "Trying: $url"
  STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -L "$url" --max-time 10)
  echo "  HTTP status: $STATUS"
  if [ "$STATUS" = "200" ]; then
    echo "  >>> FOUND! Downloading..."
    curl -s -L "$url" -o /tmp/sv-a39dce26ac2d462a93e579f4c4870001/libmonosgen-debug --max-time 60
    file /tmp/sv-a39dce26ac2d462a93e579f4c4870001/libmonosgen-debug
    break
  fi
done
arguments.description: Try downloading symbols from Microsoft symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Try downloading symbols from Microsoft symbol server

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to symbolicate the .NET runtime frames in this Android tombstone. The key frames are from `libmonosgen-2.0.so` with BuildId `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`.

To symbolicate these, I would need:
1. The debug symbols for `libmonosgen-2.0.so` with that specific BuildId
2. A tool like `llvm-symbolizer`, `addr2line`, or similar

The BuildId is `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`. This is a Mono runtime library from a .NET MAUI app.


Unfortunately, I don't have access to the debug symbols for this particular build of `libmonosgen-2.0.so`. To get these frames symbolicated, you'd need to source the matching debug symbols from either the .NET SDK's symbol packages, Microsoft's symbol server, or your app's build artifacts.

I'm thinking about trying Microsoft's symbol server to download the debug symbols directly. The URL format for ELF files with BuildId typically follows the pattern of `https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/<filename>/elf-buildid-<buildid>/<filename>`, where the buildid is the hex string from the binary. Let me attempt to fetch the symbols for `libmonosgen-2.0.so` using this approach.

However, I'm realizing this is likely a synthetic tombstone file with test data, so the BuildId probably doesn't correspond to an actual published binary that would be available on the symbol server. I need to think about what practical steps I can take here given those constraints. Let me proceed with this approach to symbolicate the frames using the Microsoft symbol server and standard tools.

arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; apt list --installed 2>/dev/null | grep -i llvm | head -5

# Try Microsoft symbol server - ELF buildid format
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
FILENAME="libmonosgen-2.0.so"

# Try various symbol server URL formats
echo "=== Trying symbol server ==="
for url in \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" \
  "https://symbols.nuget.org/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}" \
  "https://symbols.nuget.org/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug"; do
  echo "Trying: $url"
  STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -L "$url" --max-time 10)
  echo "  HTTP status: $STATUS"
  if [ "$STATUS" = "200" ]; then
    echo "  >>> FOUND! Downloading..."
    curl -s -L "$url" -o /tmp/sv-a39dce26ac2d462a93e579f4c4870001/libmonosgen-debug --max-time 60
    file /tmp/sv-a39dce26ac2d462a93e579f4c4870001/libmonosgen-debug
    break
  fi
done

toolArgs.command:
# Try Microsoft symbol server - ELF buildid format
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
FILENAME="libmonosgen-2.0.so"

# Try various symbol server URL formats
echo "=== Trying symbol server ==="
for url in \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" \
  "https://symbols.nuget.org/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}" \
  "https://symbols.nuget.org/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug"; do
  echo "Trying: $url"
  STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -L "$url" --max-time 10)

    echo "  >>> FOUND! Downloading..."
    curl -s -L "$url" -o /tmp/sv-a39dce26ac2d462a93e579f4c4870001/libmonosgen-debug --max-time 60
    file /tmp/sv-a39dce26ac2d462a93e579f4c4870001/libmonosgen-debug
    break
  fi
done

data.content: Got 16MB debug symbols file. Now let me symbolicate the frames.

arguments.command:
cd /tmp/sv-a39dce26ac2d462a93e579f4c4870001

# llvm tools handle cross-arch; find available version
SYMBOLIZER=$(ls /usr/bin/llvm-symbolizer-* 2>/dev/null | sort -V | tail -1)
ADDR2LINE=$(ls /usr/bin/llvm-addr2line-* 2>/dev/null | sort -V | tail -1)
echo "symbolizer: $SYMBOLIZER"
echo "addr2line: $ADDR2LINE"

# The PC addresses from the tombstone backtrace (libmonosgen-2.0.so frames)
ADDRS="0x222098 0x2241ac 0x1abc80 0x1abd14 0x16dfa4 0x16db6c 0x16de04"

echo ""
echo "=== Using llvm-symbolizer ==="
for addr in $ADDRS; do
  echo "--- Address $addr ---"
  echo "$addr" | $SYMBOLIZER --obj=libmonosgen-debug-sym --output-style=GNU -f -C 2>&1
done

data.reasoningText:
I have the debug symbols file (16MB, with debug_info, not stripped). Now I need to use `addr2line` to symbolicate the addresses from the tombstone. But this is an ARM64 ELF file and I'm running on x86_64, so I need a cross-architecture tool. Let me check if I have `aarch64-linux-gnu-addr2line` or if `llvm-addr2line` (which is architecture-agnostic) is available.

The addresses from the tombstone are:
- #01 pc 0x222098
- #02 pc 0x2241ac


- #03 pc 0x1abc80
- #04 pc 0x1abd14
- #05 pc 0x16dfa4
- #06 pc 0x16db6c
- #07 pc 0x16de04

I'll use llvm-symbolizer since it can handle cross-architecture symbolication without needing architecture-specific binutils.
